description: FutSpring Daily Lifecycle — full session flow from creation through attendance, team sorting, result entry, finalization, and stats display

userStories.title: Extend Daily entity and create Team entity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to extend the Daily stub entity with attendance/team fields and create the Team entity so the daily lifecycle can be tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend existing Daily entity (backend/src/main/java/com/futspring/backend/entity/Daily.java) with: confirmedPlayers (ManyToMany<User>, join table daily_confirmed_players), isFinished (boolean, default false), championImage (String, nullable)

Create Team JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), daily (ManyToOne Daily, required), name (String, required), players (ManyToMany<User>, join table team_players), @Builder.Default players = new HashSet<>()
Create TeamRepository extending JpaRepository<Team, Long> with method: findByDaily(Daily daily)
Hibernate DDL auto creates team and daily_confirmed_players/team_players join tables
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Match and PlayerMatchStat entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Match and PlayerMatchStat entities to store post-session match results and per-player goals/assists.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Match JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), daily (ManyToOne Daily, required), team1 (ManyToOne Team, required), team2 (ManyToOne Team, required), team1Score (Integer, nullable), team2Score (Integer, nullable), winner (ManyToOne Team, nullable)
Create PlayerMatchStat JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), match (ManyToOne Match, required), user (ManyToOne User, required), goals (int, default 0), assists (int, default 0)
Create MatchRepository extending JpaRepository<Match, Long> with method: findByDaily(Daily daily)
Create PlayerMatchStatRepository extending JpaRepository<PlayerMatchStat, Long> with method: findByMatch(Match match)
Hibernate DDL auto creates matches and player_match_stats tables
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create UserDailyStats, LeagueTableEntry, and DailyAward entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need aggregate result entities to store per-user daily stats, team standings, and award winners after finalization.
acceptanceCriteria: Create UserDailyStats JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), daily (ManyToOne Daily, required), user (ManyToOne User, required), goals (int, default 0), assists (int, default 0), matchesPlayed (int, default 0), wins (int, default 0)

Create LeagueTableEntry JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), daily (ManyToOne Daily, required), team (ManyToOne Team, required), position (int, default 0), wins (int, default 0), draws (int, default 0), losses (int, default 0), goalsFor (int, default 0), goalsAgainst (int, default 0), points (int, default 0)

Create DailyAward JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), daily (ManyToOne Daily, required, unique so one award record per daily), puskasWinner (ManyToOne User, nullable), wiltballWinner (ManyToOne User, nullable)
Create UserDailyStatsRepository extending JpaRepository<UserDailyStats, Long> with: findByDaily(Daily daily)
Create LeagueTableEntryRepository extending JpaRepository<LeagueTableEntry, Long> with: findByDailyOrderByPositionAsc(Daily daily)
Create DailyAwardRepository extending JpaRepository<DailyAward, Long> with: findByDaily(Daily daily)
Hibernate DDL auto creates the three tables
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend create daily + list dailies for pelada
userStories.description: As a developer, I need endpoints to create a daily manually and list all dailies for a pelada so the frontend can show history and allow ad-hoc creation.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/peladas/:peladaId/dailies — body: { dailyDate (LocalDate, required), dailyTime (String, required) }; admin only (403 for non-admins); creates Daily with status SCHEDULED; returns DailyListItemDTO
GET /api/v1/peladas/:peladaId/dailies — returns list of DailyListItemDTO for all dailies of the pelada sorted by dailyDate descending; members only (403 for non-members)
DailyListItemDTO fields: id, dailyDate, dailyTime, status, confirmedPlayerCount (size of confirmedPlayers set), isFinished
CreateDailyRequestDTO with @Valid: dailyDate (@NotNull), dailyTime (@NotBlank)
DailyService created with createDaily(peladaId, request, userEmail) and getDailiesForPelada(peladaId, userEmail) methods annotated @Transactional
DailyController at /api/v1 with POST /peladas/{peladaId}/dailies and GET /peladas/{peladaId}/dailies endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend get daily detail endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a GET /api/v1/dailies/:id endpoint returning full daily state so the frontend can render the daily page.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/dailies/:id — members of the parent pelada only (403 otherwise); returns DailyDetailDTO

DailyDetailDTO fields: id, dailyDate, dailyTime, status, isFinished, championImage, confirmedPlayers (List of PlayerDTO: id, username, image, stars, position), teams (List of TeamDTO: id, name, totalStars, players List<PlayerDTO>), matches (List of MatchDTO: id, team1Id, team1Name, team2Id, team2Name, team1Score, team2Score, winnerId), playerStats (List of UserDailyStatsDTO: userId, username, goals, assists, matchesPlayed, wins), leagueTable (List of LeagueTableEntryDTO: teamId, teamName, position, wins, draws, losses, goalsFor, goalsAgainst, goalDiff, points), award (AwardDTO: puskasWinnerId, puskasWinnerName, wiltballWinnerId, wiltballWinnerName), peladaId, peladaName
totalStars on TeamDTO is computed as sum of stars of all players in that team
goalDiff on LeagueTableEntryDTO is goalsFor - goalsAgainst
GET /api/v1/dailies/:id added to DailyController; DailyService has getDailyDetail(id, userEmail) @Transactional(readOnly = true)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend attendance endpoints (confirm and disconfirm)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need endpoints for players to confirm and disconfirm attendance so the admin knows who's coming.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/dailies/:id/confirm — adds current user to daily.confirmedPlayers; user must be a member of the parent pelada (403 otherwise); returns 409 if already confirmed; returns 400 if daily status is IN_COURSE, FINISHED, or CANCELED
DELETE /api/v1/dailies/:id/confirm — removes current user from daily.confirmedPlayers; returns 400 if not confirmed or if daily status is IN_COURSE, FINISHED, or CANCELED
Both endpoints return updated DailyListItemDTO (id, status, confirmedPlayerCount)
DailyService has confirmAttendance(id, userEmail) and disconfirmAttendance(id, userEmail) methods annotated @Transactional
DailyController handles POST /dailies/{id}/confirm and DELETE /dailies/{id}/confirm
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend sort teams + swap players endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need endpoints to auto-balance teams from confirmed players and swap players between teams so admin can manage fair matchups.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/dailies/:id/sort-teams — admin only; requires at least 2 confirmed players; idempotent: deletes existing teams before recreating; greedy balancing algorithm: sort confirmed players by stars descending, assign each to team with lowest current totalStars (snake-draft), default 2 teams for MVP; returns updated DailyDetailDTO teams field; returns 400 if status is IN_COURSE, FINISHED, or CANCELED

PUT /api/v1/dailies/:id/teams/swap — body: { player1Id: Long, player2Id: Long }; admin only; swaps two players between their respective teams; both players must be on a team in this daily (400 otherwise); persists swap and returns updated teams list
DailyService has sortTeams(id, userEmail) and swapPlayers(id, player1Id, player2Id, userEmail) @Transactional methods
DailyController handles POST /dailies/{id}/sort-teams and PUT /dailies/{id}/teams/swap
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend daily status management (advance and cancel)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint to advance or cancel the daily status so the system reflects what is actually happening.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/v1/dailies/:id/status — body: { status: String }; admin only
Valid transitions: SCHEDULED → CONFIRMED, SCHEDULED → CANCELED, CONFIRMED → IN_COURSE, CONFIRMED → CANCELED; return 400 for any other transition
Returns updated DailyListItemDTO with new status
DailyService has updateStatus(id, newStatus, userEmail) @Transactional method
DailyController handles PUT /dailies/{id}/status
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend batch submit match results
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint to batch create or overwrite match results and per-player stats so the admin can enter post-session data.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/dailies/:id/results — admin only; daily must have status IN_COURSE (400 otherwise)

Request body: List of MatchResultDTO { matchId (Long, nullable), team1Id (Long), team2Id (Long), team1Score (int, min 0), team2Score (int, min 0), playerStats: List of PlayerStatInputDTO { userId, goals (int, default 0, min 0), assists (int, default 0, min 0) } }
If matchId is provided and exists, overwrite that match's scores; if null or not found, create new Match
Derive winner: team with higher score; null if draw
Save or overwrite PlayerMatchStat records for each userId listed; any confirmed player not listed defaults to goals=0, assists=0 for that match
Returns updated list of MatchDTO
DailyService has submitResults(id, List<MatchResultDTO>, userEmail) @Transactional method
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend finalize daily with stats and league table computation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a finalize endpoint that computes UserDailyStats, the league table, and records awards then marks the daily as FINISHED.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/dailies/:id/finalize — body: { puskasWinnerId: Long, wiltballWinnerId: Long }; admin only; daily must be IN_COURSE with at least one match result (400 otherwise); both winner IDs must be confirmed players (400 otherwise)

Computation (all in one @Transactional method): (1) UserDailyStats per confirmed player: aggregate goals and assists from PlayerMatchStat records across all matches of this daily; matchesPlayed = number of matches that player appeared in; wins = number of those matches where the player's team was the winner; delete existing UserDailyStats for this daily before recomputing; (2) LeagueTableEntry per team: from match results compute W/D/L/GF/GA; points = win*3 + draw*1; sort by points desc then GD desc; set position field; delete existing LeagueTableEntry for this daily before recomputing; (3) DailyAward: create or update DailyAward for this daily with puskasWinnerId and wiltballWinnerId
Set daily.status = FINISHED and daily.isFinished = true
Returns updated DailyDetailDTO with all fields populated
DailyService has finalizeDaily(id, puskasWinnerId, wiltballWinnerId, userEmail) @Transactional method
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend champion image upload endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint to upload a champion group photo after finalization so the admin can commemorate the session.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/v1/dailies/:id/champion-image — multipart/form-data; admin only; daily must be FINISHED (400 otherwise)
Store file under uploads/ with UUID prefix (same pattern as pelada image upload); max 5MB; JPEG/PNG/WebP only
Updates daily.championImage field with stored filename
Returns updated DailyListItemDTO
DailyService has uploadChampionImage(id, file, userEmail) @Transactional method
DailyController handles PUT /dailies/{id}/champion-image with @RequestParam("file") MultipartFile
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend API layer and TypeScript types for dailies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript interfaces and API functions for all daily endpoints so the frontend can call the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/types/daily.ts with interfaces: PlayerDTO { id, username, image, stars, position }, TeamDTO { id, name, totalStars, players: PlayerDTO[] }, MatchDTO { id, team1Id, team1Name, team2Id, team2Name, team1Score, team2Score, winnerId }, UserDailyStatsDTO { userId, username, goals, assists, matchesPlayed, wins }, LeagueTableEntryDTO { teamId, teamName, position, wins, draws, losses, goalsFor, goalsAgainst, goalDiff, points }, AwardDTO { puskasWinnerId, puskasWinnerName, wiltballWinnerId, wiltballWinnerName }, DailyListItem { id, dailyDate, dailyTime, status, confirmedPlayerCount, isFinished }, DailyDetail { id, dailyDate, dailyTime, status, isFinished, championImage, confirmedPlayers: PlayerDTO[], teams: TeamDTO[], matches: MatchDTO[], playerStats: UserDailyStatsDTO[], leagueTable: LeagueTableEntryDTO[], award: AwardDTO | null, peladaId, peladaName }

Create src/api/dailies.ts with functions: getDailiesForPelada(peladaId), getDailyDetail(id), createDaily(peladaId, data), confirmAttendance(id), disconfirmAttendance(id), sortTeams(id), swapPlayers(id, player1Id, player2Id), updateDailyStatus(id, status), submitResults(id, results), finalizeDaily(id, puskasWinnerId, wiltballWinnerId), uploadChampionImage(id, file)
All functions use existing Axios client (src/api/client.ts)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend daily history list on Pelada page and Create Daily modal
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to create a daily; as a member, I want to see the history of dailies on the pelada page.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'Sessions' section below the members list on PeladaDetailPage that fetches and displays getDailiesForPelada(peladaId)
Each daily row shows: dailyDate (formatted), dailyTime, status badge (colored: SCHEDULED=blue, CONFIRMED=yellow, IN_COURSE=green, FINISHED=gray, CANCELED=red), confirmedPlayerCount
Each row links to /daily/:id
Skeleton loading state while fetching (3 skeleton rows)
Empty state: 'No sessions yet.' if no dailies
Admin-only 'Create Session' button in the Sessions section header; clicking opens a modal with fields: dailyDate (date input, required) and dailyTime (time input, required)
On create success: close modal, refetch dailies list, show success toast
Submit button shows loading state; error toast on failure
Add /daily/:id route to App.tsx with lazy-loaded DailyDetailPage (placeholder page for now)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend Daily detail page — base structure and attendance
userStories.description: As a member, I want to see the daily detail and confirm or disconfirm my attendance.
acceptanceCriteria: Create DailyDetailPage at /daily/:id that fetches getDailyDetail(id); replace the placeholder from US-013
Header shows: pelada name (links to /pelada/:peladaId), date, time, status badge
Confirmed Players section: list of player avatars/names with count; skeleton while loading
Non-member accessing route sees 'Access Denied' (detect 403, no crash)
'Confirm' button shown to the current user when daily status is SCHEDULED or CONFIRMED and user is not yet in confirmedPlayers; calls confirmAttendance(id) on click
'Disconfirm' button shown when current user is already in confirmedPlayers and status is SCHEDULED or CONFIRMED; calls disconfirmAttendance(id) on click
Both buttons are hidden when status is IN_COURSE, FINISHED, or CANCELED
Confirmed players list updates immediately after confirm/disconfirm (refetch detail)
Toast shown on success and error for both actions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend admin team management — sort and swap
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to sort confirmed players into balanced teams and then manually swap players between teams.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only 'Sort Teams' button on DailyDetailPage visible when status is SCHEDULED or CONFIRMED and confirmedPlayerCount >= 2; calls sortTeams(id) on click; shows loading state
After sorting, teams are displayed as cards: team name, total stars, list of players (avatar/name/stars)
If teams already exist (daily loaded with teams), they are always shown
'Re-sort Teams' label when teams already exist; clicking re-runs sort (replaces existing teams)

Each player in a team card has a 'Move' button (admin only); clicking a player selects them; then clicking another player in a different team calls swapPlayers(id, p1Id, p2Id) and refetches; cancel selection on same player click
Teams update without full page reload after swap
Toast on sort success and swap success; error toast on failure
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend status advancement and cancel daily
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to advance the daily status or cancel it so the system reflects reality.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only status action bar shown on DailyDetailPage based on current status: SCHEDULED → shows 'Confirm Daily' button (advances to CONFIRMED) and 'Cancel' button; CONFIRMED → shows 'Start Session' button (advances to IN_COURSE) and 'Cancel' button; IN_COURSE and later → no advance buttons
Each action opens a confirmation dialog with descriptive text before proceeding
On confirm: calls updateDailyStatus(id, newStatus); status badge updates immediately on success; toast on success and error
Cancel action advances status to CANCELED; page shows CANCELED badge; status action bar disappears
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend results entry form (admin, post-session)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to enter match scores and player stats after the session so the results can be recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only 'Enter Results' button on DailyDetailPage when status is IN_COURSE; opens a results entry modal/panel
Results form shows one row per match (based on teams: if 2 teams, 1 match shown; admin can add more matches with 'Add Match' button showing additional team1/team2 selects from available teams)
Each match row: team1 name + score input (number, min 0), team2 name + score input (number, min 0)
Below each match: expandable player stats section — for each confirmed player, inputs for goals (default 0) and assists (default 0)
If results already exist (daily.matches populated), form is pre-filled with existing data
Submit calls submitResults(id, results); shows loading state; toast on success/error; closes modal on success and refetches daily detail
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend finalization flow — select awards and finalize
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to select Puskas and Wiltball award winners and finalize the daily so stats are computed and the session is officially closed.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only 'Finalize Daily' button on DailyDetailPage when status is IN_COURSE and daily.matches is non-empty; opens finalization modal
Finalization modal shows two selects: 'Puskas Award' and 'Wiltball Award', each populated with confirmedPlayers; both required
Submit button disabled until both selects have a value
On submit: calls finalizeDaily(id, puskasWinnerId, wiltballWinnerId); shows loading state
On success: close modal, show success toast, refetch daily detail (page now shows FINISHED state)
Error toast on failure; modal stays open on error
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend finished daily view — results, league table, stats, and awards
userStories.description: As a player, I want to view all results, standings, stats, and awards of a finished daily session.
acceptanceCriteria: When daily.status is FINISHED, DailyDetailPage shows additional sections below the teams display:
Match Results section: each match shows team1 name vs team2 name with scores; winning team name is bold; draw displayed as 'Draw'
League Table section: table with columns Position, Team, W, D, L, GF, GA, GD, Points; sorted by position; winning team highlighted
Player Stats section: table with columns Player, Goals, Assists, Matches, Wins; sorted by goals desc
Awards section: shows Puskas Award winner and Wiltball Award winner with name and avatar

Champion Image section: if daily.championImage is set, show the image; admin sees 'Upload Champion Photo' button that opens a file picker and calls uploadChampionImage(); success toast and refetch on upload
All sections use skeleton placeholders while initial data is loading
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
